user_query:
We need a polished WeChat Official Account article for our productivity SaaS (FlowGrid) on reducing meeting overload in remote teams. Please read the following to tailor voice, structure, and substance:
- input/brief.json (purpose, tone, length target, desired action)
- input/audience_persona.md (who we're writing for and their pains)
- input/notes.txt (must-include data points and the NovaCore case)
- input/keywords.csv (phrases to naturally weave in)
- input/style_guide.md (voice and style constraints)

Deliverables:
1) Write the full article to output/article.md using a "How-to" style title that starts with "How to ...". Target 1,200–1,600 words. Follow a clear structure: Opening (hook, bridge, promise, preview), Body with exactly 3 key points (each point must have a subheading, an explanation, a concrete example, and one actionable tip), and a Closing (fresh-summary + strong call-to-action + a discussion prompt question). Lead with the main takeaway in the opening (Pyramid Principle). Use second-person "you" throughout and include emojis tastefully; avoid markdown tables. Incorporate at least 4 of the provided keywords from input/keywords.csv. Include the NovaCore case from the notes and the specific data point mentioned.
2) Create a high-level outline of the three body points (just the three point titles, one per line prefixed with "- ") and save it to output/outline.txt.
3) Generate output/metadata.json with: title, tone, purpose, audience, keywords_used (array), word_count (int), and reading_time_minutes (int). Ensure tone and purpose reflect input/brief.json.

Make the closing invite readers to Follow and Comment (and optionally Share). Include a Discussion prompt at the end with a question that encourages real-world experiences. Keep everything in English and suitable for WeChat formatting.
